extra.text: The realm remembers every path you choose.
unified starting point for combat, magic, exploration, crafting, hunting, survival, and dragon-tier paths
The first swing teaches the second where to land.
Hold the line when the world starts pushing back.
. Blocking knocks back and slows melee attackers.
A heavy blade should feel inevitable.
. Axe and heavy hits cleave nearby enemies.
Simply Swords and heavy Better Combat patterns
Armor is not an excuse to stand still.
Make armor panic before the wearer does.
The same blade can ask three different questions.
Hit the stance, not the body.
Let the monster learn what commitment feels like.
Every block becomes a promise to answer.
Keep the chain alive until the room goes quiet.
The best retreat is a wall that walks forward.
Some weapons remember the boss before you meet it.
Your timing becomes the party metronome.
The line breaks after you do, which is to say never.
Make tall things discover the ground.
. Bonus damage scales with the target's max health.
Steel, stance, and nerve become one clean answer.
Hit the same guard until it cracks.
Repeated melee hits on one enemy build Guard; at
The next kill is fed by the last.
On kill: gain Speed I and Strength I for
The wound is where the fury lives.
HP gain scaling Resistance and melee damage, stronger again under
Each kill without taking a heavy hit stacks escalating Strength and Speed (up to
); a hard hit resets the streak.
Move before the blade decides where you are.
A light weapon wins by asking faster questions.
The ground is just another escape route.
. Hard landings deal an area thud scaled by fall height.
Who says there is no luck?
Exit danger with enough breath to punish it.
The chest opens because it knows better.
. Humanoid kills sometimes drop extra loot.
One target, one answer, no wasted motion.
Arrive in the blind spot and leave a rumor.
Treasure has a sound if you are greedy enough.
The safest enemy is the one that never turns around.
A bad moment is a doorway if you are fast enough.
The world drops better loot when it respects you.
Risk becomes rhythm when the fight gets close.
Hit first, hit clean, and do not be there for the answer.
The right hands find the impossible things.
Your hits stack Vulnerability on a target:
damage and mark the target with Glowing for
Killing a marked enemy empowers your next hit for
health take heavy bonus damage from you.
Tracks are promises written by things with teeth.
The wild gives away more than it means to.
Name the threat and it gets smaller.
. Arrows briefly pin (slow) what they hit.
A good companion turns distance into courage.
. Your tamed pets regenerate while near you.
Light armor should disappear into the brush.
Every scar is a map to the next hunt.
. Rare and boss kills drop bonus loot and XP.
The bite is less frightening when you know the animal.
Hold still until the world lines up.
When the big shadow moves, you already moved.
Bring down anything arrogant enough to fly.
The pack hunts like one nervous system.
The realm has many monsters and one answer.
You know which ruins are lying about being empty.
Your ranged hits mark a target:
ranged damage to it and Glowing for
Arrows deal more damage the farther they travel, up to
Consecutive hits on your marked target stack
Your arrows burst on impact for a small area blast.
A small spell is still a door opening.
Spend power like you expect to survive.
A ward is a polite refusal to die.
The blade is a wand with better manners.
. Melee hits sometimes unleash a spell.
Mend the wound before it becomes a story.
. Bonus damage vs undead; undead kills heal you.
. Your hits chill and slow the target.
Lightning is a shortcut with opinions.
A robe is armor if the math is mean enough.
Carry enough silence to answer any room.
The circle closes and the body remembers.
Ice and lightning agree only when you insist.
Let hostile magic break itself on the way in.
Cast through the swing, then swing through the spell.
The book opens because it recognizes the hand.
Night is not darkness. It is fuel.
Some doors are not built. They are convinced.
spell and magic damage against armored or magic-resistant enemies.
Slaying foes restores a burst of mana and briefly raises spell power.
A full font spills into a shield.
While mana is full, gain a regenerating arcane shield (Absorption).
Your hits sometimes arc lightning to a nearby enemy.
Ore, gear, and ruin all have the same grammar.
A clean bench saves more lives than a sharp sword.
The machine works better when you do not trip over it.
No expedition survives on courage alone.
. Eating grants a burst of Haste and Speed.
If the numbers are right, the wall is temporary.
A boat is a base that learned courage.
. While mounted or boating you are fall-immune and faster.
Patch the kit before the kit becomes the problem.
Walk the line like you own every gear.
Load faster, aim calmer, leave less wall.
Create Big Cannons and boss armor
A good meal is applied logistics.
Old stone pays rent when you know where to hit it.
. Mining ore sometimes yields bonus raw ore.
The workshop becomes a second nervous system.
Some problems need an answer measured in recoil.
Nothing heroic happens without someone moving the supplies.
A home is a machine for surviving tomorrow.
Breaking ore and stone has a chance to yield bonus salvage.
While wearing armor, gain steady Damage Resistance.
When it breaks, it breaks outward.
A heavy hit triggers a steam burst: knock back nearby enemies and gain Absorption,
Hit the wall, the wall hits back.
A portion of melee damage you take is reflected as a steam burst.
The sky tells the truth before the logs do.
. Rain and storms grant you speed.
Cold bites less when you bite back.
. In snowy biomes you shrug off cold and gain Resistance.
Pack enough food to make panic optional.
Every mountain is just a longer stair.
Ruins do not hide treasure. They test manners.
A small fire and steady hands can reset a disaster.
The dark is a room, not an enemy.
Let the year turn without taking you with it.
Bad weather is just cover with thunder.
Every board has a job. Every job has a road.
A good camp turns night into planning time.
The fall starts lying before your foot slips.
The map gets smaller when you stop arguing with it.
Home is wherever you can recover before sunrise.
The farther you go, the better prepared you become.
The harsh world wastes its teeth on you.
blocks slowly mend while you stand with them.
Near fire or a campfire, gain Regeneration and a small temporary health boost.

HP with brief Resistance, if you carry food or a survival item.
Dropping low (once per cooldown) surges a heal and clears the bad stuff.
The old fire knows when you are serious.
Armor should remember the element that made it.
. Heavy hits briefly grant fire and ice immunity.
Do not fight the legend. Fight the joints.
. Bonus damage vs dragons and large flying foes.
The arena feels wrong before the music starts.
The void can stare. It does not get to take you.
A mount is not transport. It is a pact.
. Your mount takes less damage and shares your Resistance.
When the room becomes a boss fight, breathe first.
Fire is a language. Learn the grammar.
Cold loses its courage around old blood.
Boss armor is only temporary confidence.
A pact with a dragon should change the weather in your chest.
The oldest things in the world learn your name late.
Stories stop being warnings after you survive them.
The realm makes room when you arrive.
Dealing and taking damage charges a Draconic meter; at full, your next strike erupts with elemental force.
Bigger is just a longer fall.
damage to enemies tougher than you, including bosses and elites.
Become the thing in the panicked books.
When your Draconic meter fills, ascend for
s: Resistance, Strength, Regeneration, and an elemental aura,
The old blood answers the moon.
On full-moon nights you gain lifesteal and bonus damage.
